message:
feat(evaluator): integrate sanitize_for_pattern_matching for false positive immunity

git_safety_guard-fpim: Implement false positive reduction for string arguments

This commit integrates the execution-context-aware sanitization into
the command evaluation pipeline, eliminating false positives from
dangerous-looking substrings inside commit messages, search patterns,
issue descriptions, and other data contexts.

## Problem Solved

Previously, commands like these were incorrectly blocked:
- `git commit -m "Fix detection of dangerous patterns"`
- `bd create --description="This blocks dangerous commands"`
- `grep "dangerous pattern" file.txt`

The sanitizer strips known-safe string arguments before pattern matching,
so dangerous substrings inside DATA don't trigger EXECUTION blocking.

## Changes

### src/evaluator.rs
- Import `sanitize_for_pattern_matching` from context module
- Add sanitization step after quick-reject, before normalization
- Re-run keyword gate on sanitized command if content was stripped
- Apply to both `evaluate_command` and `evaluate_command_with_legacy`

### src/main.rs
- Integrate sanitization into hook mode pipeline
- Mark legacy SAFE_PATTERNS/DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS as test-only (#[cfg(test)])
- This prepares for their eventual removal (git_safety_guard-99e.3.4)

### src/context.rs
- Formatting improvements (rustfmt)
- New test: `sanitize_strips_bd_description_value`

## Performance

- Sanitization is O(n) single-pass tokenization
- Uses Cow<str> to avoid allocation when no changes needed
- Re-runs quick-reject on sanitized command for fast path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
